query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "learning from the self-future: on-policy self-distillation for dllms" linguistics
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (d) as a foundational methodology reference for Diffusion Large Language Models (DLLMs), which is the core technical domain of the user's question regarding "dLLMs" and self-distillation. Additionally, it addresses criterion (b) by providing empirical evidence on the behavior of DLLMs (the dependent variable) in generation tasks, which is essential context for any follow-up work on extending their capabilities.
